channel.title: Posts on hiddenbyte.log
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on hiddenbyte.log

item.title: .march.2026.photo.dump
10 years of Porto
item.description:
This month marks 10 years since I moved to Porto, and I can say that this city feels like home.

This doesn’t mean I feel like a Tripeiro, nor do I feel like an Alfacinha (for those who know me, I was born and raised in Lisbon). Fuck, sometimes I don’t even feel like a Portuguese. But I definitely feel that I am at the right place 💙

item.title: Nostalgia
item.description:
Nostalgia is counter to the natural flow of time. The arrow of time moves forward, yet nostalgia pulls us backward, creating resistance to the present and future. Thus, nostalgia is a feeling that is not in harmony with the natural flow of time.

This does not mean we should forget the past or any feelings, whether bad or good. But we should not become attached to the past—being attached to the past is what nostalgia is—and we should let go of the past.

item.title: "Life is not meant to be rushed through"
item.description:
life is not meant to be rushed through. It is not a race, nor is it a problem to be solved. It is an experience to be lived, and living well requires presence.

in the quiet art of attention

item.title: Digging into Goroutines I
item.description:
Go enables writing programs with concurrent threads of execution. And it does so by introducing the concept of goroutine.

A goroutine has a simple model: it is a function executing concurrently with other goroutines in the same address space.

in Effective Go

Before digging into goroutines, having an understanding on “how” the Go compiler and runtime work together in order to launch a goroutine will pave the path to its internals.
